title: rubus griesiae
intro: Rubus griesiae, also known as the gray raspberry or gray thimbleberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Rubus griesiae are often made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
